IBEW.text:
**Trampin the IBEW Indoor Locals — Friday, July 17, 2026**

**LU-494 Milwaukee, WI** is on fire right now — three contractors (Gurtz, MJ Electric, Cupertino) hammering the Port Washington Data Center with **JW scale plus $10/hr base bump** across 60+ calls. That's real money stacking on top of your ticket. Book depth is thin; they're chasing bodies hard.

**LU-481 Indianapolis, IN** is the heavyweight play. Meade Electric and Fluor Constructors both paying **$53.80/hr — 10% over scale** — on Meta and Eli Lilly construction in Lebanon. Google Data Center in Mooresville (Ermco, 24 calls) needs OSHA 10 but runs new build rates. Forty-three calls, under 200 hands on the board means Book 1 is moving.

**LU-26 Washington, DC** has FIS dropping 36 calls on the 48920 VA Data Centers (Sterling, VA) with **$125/day per diem**, active data center work, and planned OT already baked in. That's the East Coast data center play — low book depth, solid margin, no guessing on hours.

**LU-237 Niagara Falls, NY** is Lake Mariner Data center city: Ferguson (51 calls) and O'Connell (27 calls) both 7/10 schedules with per diem, orientation locked in Mon-Tue-Thu. Sixty-some hands on the board means you're getting on.

**LU-583 El Paso, TX** moves work fast — Summit Line and Rosendin across 49 and 27 calls respectively, **$55/hr base, all OT double time**, NM State JIW License required. Tight board (under 110 hands total), high turnover play.

**LU-291 Boise, ID** (Micron ID1) is the PLA grind: **$45.33/hr plus $170/day per diem x 7 days**, 4-10 and 5-8 splits, incentives running **$20/hr extra** on some calls. Forty-eight calls but only 92 hands means consistent flow if you can stomach the wage structure.

Data center builds are absorbing the board — AWS, Google, Meta, Microsoft, and QTS facilities are the work magnet right now, pulling scale or near-scale across every region. If you're mobile, you're employed.

LINEMAN.text:
**Trampin the IBEW Lineman Locals — Friday, July 17, 2026**

**LU-71 Columbus, OH** — 38 calls, 118 hands deep. NEW RIVER ELECT is running the show: **$61.44/hr** on 36 journeyman lineman doing distribution work in Columbus proper (6-10s, **$125/day PD plus $100 bonus**), plus nine more heads south in 4-10 optional shifts. JJM POWER is tighter—five JLs in Chillicothe on south central distro, 4-10s, **$100/day**, 4-man crew. This is bread-and-butter distro work, not flash, but the board's moving.

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** — 25 calls, 85 hands. Parsons Electric dominates with 40 combined calls on the Polaris Forge Data Center in Harwood—**$44.80/hr** with **$100/day non-taxed PD** Monday-Friday and **$150 Sat/Sun**. Requires ND license or reciprocal. Hunt Electric's got 10 more on the Air Base, same scale, 4-10s with Friday OT. Data center work's hot right now—long-term, steady, and the per diem stacks if you work the weekend.

**LU-291 Boise, ID** — 22 calls, 92 hands. VECA Electric and Quality Electric are locked into the Micron ID1 PLA—**$45.33/hr** but **$170/day PD** (if eligible) across a 60-60-50 schedule. That's nearly **$850/week in per diem alone**. Day shift 6 a.m. starts, 4-10s. Prime Controls has six more. Book's deep but the per diem math here is unbeatable for a long seasonal push.

**LU-474 Memphis, TN** — 4 calls, 58 hands. ArchKey Sachs is hiring 45 JWs on the Google Data Center in West Memphis, AR at **$36.65/hr** plus **$125/day incentive** for full shift. Day shift, 4-10s and 2-8s, requires Arkansas license. Volume play on a major tech build.

**LU-1547 Anchorage, AK** — 41 calls, only 60 hands. Most work is "Other" classifications (telecom, maintenance, labor)—actual journeyman lineman calls are sparse. Alaska Communications pays **$45.31/hr** telecom but it's thin meat.

Data centers and long-duration PLA projects are consolidating the board; 411 calls across 56 locals means hands are clustering around Micron, Google, and Polaris Forge.

UA.text:
**Trampin the UA Locals — Friday, July 17, 2026**

**LU-140 Salt Lake City, UT** is running hot with 28 calls chasing 105 hands—the fattest board in the pack. BCS Mechanical has 34 slots open on West Valley work (Journeyman Plumber), plus another 10 apprentice spots. P.C. is staffing the Delta Center project in Salt Lake City with 7 more calls. DSI's Draper shop needs TIG-capable pipefitters. No scale posted, but this is volume play—lots of hands moving through fast.

**LU-208 Denver, CO** has 21 calls but only ~34 hands in the funnel, so book's moving. Kiewit's Fort St. Vrain Powerhouse gig pays **$65/day per diem** for welders on nights (5/9's, 5pm-2am, paid 9.5 hours). Murphy's fab shop fitter role runs 6/10's nights with shift differential. Olson Plumbing's Parker hospital job is straight day 4/10's for pipefitters. Night shift premium work concentrated here.

**LU-213 St. John, NB** is pure industrial—15 calls at Stuart Olson's Irving Oil Refinery under GPMA Agreement (day shift), plus another 7 calls split between Irving Oil and the Irving Pulp & Paper Mill in Lorneville. Night shift 4x10's available on the paper mill side. Journeyman Pipefitter work, some marked short-term. Book depth tight at ~29 hands across 9 calls.

**LU-776 Lima, OH** has shutdown work stacked: Nutrien fab shop through August 3 (6/10's, **$11/hr ST premium + $16.50/hr OT/DT** or **$150/day per diem over 50 miles**). Cenovus maintenance pulls 5/8's with spotty OT. Nooter's fab gig pays **$125/day worked** incentive. All-combo welder testing required. Book at ~22 hands; shutdown premium driving bodies east.

**LU-192 Cheyenne, WY** reads small (3 calls, 18 hands) but punches hard: MAP's Holly Frontier Sinclair Refinery shutdown needs 6 slots (welders + fitters, 7/10's day). PMI's Ellsworth AFB and Fort Meade VA gigs offer **$100/day per diem** in South Dakota. OSHA 10 and background check mandatory. Short-term, high-premium work.

**LU-525 Las Vegas, NV** is leisure plumbing—Hansen and Bombard pushing bodies into the Venetian, Palazzo, and Encore. Encore swing shift (2am–12:30pm, dropping to 40 after ramp). Thin per diem data, but steady casino work.

Industrial turnarounds and refinery shutdowns are pulling hands South and East; Salt Lake's volume game is the counterweight out West.
